title: biscutella laevigata subsp. megacarpaea
intro: Biscutella laevigata subsp. megacarpaea is a plant species belonging to the family Brassicaceae, also known as the mustard family, and is native to the Iberian Peninsula. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional herbal medicine, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of Biscutella laevigata subsp. megacarpaea can be found in the form of infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
